reviews.review_msg: A hard class made worse by Reid's extremely bad grade allocation. Barely getting by with a C- should not happen when you put in hours of effort each week only to find out his exams contain completely unfair questions and the grading scheme involves examinations only.

Ignore the reviews slandering an amazing prof due to students' own faults. I didn't do too well in this class in the end, and still recognize what a wonderful prof Reid is. Absolute gem. Makes uninteresting content (to me) fun. Really wants you to succeed. Hardest part of this class is that your grade is 100% based on 5 exams: 3 lab, 2 lecture.

As someone who took both 202 and 206 this semester, I am appalled to see the comments that this specific student is leaving for all of the easc professors. Reid has been BY FAR the best prof I've had at sfu, and I very much look forward to future classes with him! He is very groovy, don't let the nasty comments misdirect you.
Needs to lecture better, every lecture is hard to understand and nobody can do well on the exams because of it.

Mineralogy is a hard course and Reid acknowledges it but he needs to do a far better job providing help to students. He gives literally no information about what the lab exams are like other than"memorize these minerals". When the lab exams come you literally have no choice but to guess even when you've studied everything.also reads slides too much
All the previous reviews with ratings similar to mine say enough.

Kevin's a good teacher but he has a kind of jaded attitude towards students. Attendance seriously matters, he gives handouts that are not posted online. The textbook is used regularly, and although there is a LOT of (sometimes challenging) content to cover, I think he teaches it well. If you take 202 with him, just be prepared to work hard.

I have no positives about this guy as a prof. He can't teach and relies on the textbook and other classes to do the teaching for him. He is condescending towards his students and has spoken poorly of other profs. He has an ego and blames students for their study habits instead of his own teaching abilities. Avoid.

I don't care what anybody says, I love big daddy Kev. He's so funny and he tried really hard to make the most of the online learning. The lab midterm was extremely fair but the lab final was a different story... Go to office hours to go over tests and ask questions about exams! They are very hard so you have to put the effort in

Oh, where do I begin? This course is one of the most difficult in the major as it is, and it does not get any better if Kevin is teaching it to you. I know that we are in the pandemic, but that is not an excuse to pre-record some lectures and give rock samples that look like nothing we have seen in class. Avoid this class and prof.
